core_thesis: Adorno's negative dialectic denies that thought can close the gap between concept and object: the object is nonidentical with the forms that claim to master it, so philosophy must persist with contradiction rather than force reconciliation ([Negative Dialectics, Part Two](cite://adorno/negative-dialectics/2); [Minima Moralia §29](cite://adorno/minima-moralia/29)). Identity-thinking is false not because it thinks, but because it treats abstraction as dominion. Truth survives only as determinate attention to what the concept leaves wounded or unassimilated.
